event: no symptoms of dysphagia were reported
no symptoms of odynophagia were reported
preoperative pulmonary radiography and CT revealed prominent anterior thoracic osteophytes
SAPIEN 3 valve was deployed without any incident
postprocedural TEE showed a prosthesis in good position
no trauma was noticed during transesophageal probe insertion or removal
no blood was noticed during transesophageal probe insertion or removal
gastric aspiration with an orogastric tube revealed blood-tinged secretions
patient was extubated in the operating room
patient was transferred to the intensive care unit
computed tomography with intravenous contrast of the neck, thorax, and abdomen was performed
three hundred milliliters of serosanguinous liquid was drained from the pleural space
esophagogastroscopy showed a 4-cm vertical perforation of the middle third of the esophagus
patient returned to the operating room to undergo a right thoracotomy
esophageal laceration site was found approximately 1.5 cm below the arch of the azygous vein
a large vertebral osteophyte was visualized and palpated at the site of the perforation
primary closure of the esophageal wall in two layers
an intercostal muscular flap was mobilized to complete the closure
three thoracic drains were left in place
patient was transferred to the intensive care unit
postoperative period was complicated by pneumonia
congestive heart failure with pulmonary edema
patient died after withdrawal of care
